001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.StateEnum.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.util.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
037
038/**
039 * Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link JsonParser}.
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
042 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonBasics">JSON Basics</a>
047
048 * </ul>
049 */
050@SuppressWarnings({
051   "java:S110",  // Deep inheritance inherent to the serializer/parser session hierarchy
052   "java:S115",  // PROP_xxx constants use camelCase after prefix intentionally (property keys, not enum-style constants)
053   "java:S125",  // State-machine and parse-path comments (S1: ..., Let o be null) are documentation, not commented-out code
054   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for JSON parser dispatch and state machines
055   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
056   "rawtypes", // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
057   "resource" // ParserReader is managed by caller
058})
059public class JsonParserSession extends ReaderParserSession implements TokenReadable, ArrayRecordReadable {
060
061   // Property name constants
062   private static final String PROP_validateEnd = "validateEnd";
063   private static final String PROP_JsonParserSession_validateEnd = "JsonParserSession.validateEnd";
064
065   /**
066    * Builder class.
067    */
068   @SuppressWarnings({
069      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
070   })
071   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends ReaderParserSession.Builder<SELF> {
072
073      private boolean validateEnd;
074      private JsonParser ctx;
075
076      /**
077       * Constructor
078       *
079       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
080       */
081      protected Builder(JsonParser ctx) {
082         super(ctx);
083         this.ctx = ctx;
084         validateEnd = ctx.isValidateEnd();
085      }
086
087      @Override
088      public JsonParserSession build() {
089         return new JsonParserSession(this);
090      }
091
092      /**
093       * Validate end.
094       *
095       * @param value The new value for this setting.
096       * @return This object.
097       */
098      public SELF validateEnd(boolean value) {
099         validateEnd = value;
100         return self();
101      }
102
103      @Override /* Overridden from Builder */
104      public SELF property(String key, Object value) {
105         if (key == null) { super.property(key, value); return self(); }
106         switch (key) {
107            case PROP_validateEnd, PROP_JsonParserSession_validateEnd:
108               return validateEnd(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
109            default:
110               super.property(key, value);
111               return self();
112         }
113      }
114
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
119    */
120   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
121
122      DefaultBuilder(JsonParser ctx) {
123         super(ctx);
124      }
125   }
126
127   private static final AsciiSet decChars = AsciiSet.create().ranges("0-9").build();
128
129   /**
130    * Creates a new builder for this object.
131    *
132    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
133    * @return A new builder.
134    */
135   @SuppressWarnings({
136      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
137   })
138   public static Builder<?> create(JsonParser ctx) {
139      return new DefaultBuilder(ctx);
140   }
141
142   private final JsonParser ctx;
143   private final boolean validateEnd;
144
145   /**
146    * Constructor.
147    *
148    * @param builder The builder for this object.
149    */
150   protected JsonParserSession(Builder<?> builder) {
151      super(builder);
152      ctx = builder.ctx;
153      validateEnd = builder.validateEnd;
154   }
155
156   @SuppressWarnings({
157      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parser state machine
158   })
159   <T> T parseAnything(ClassMeta<?> eType, ParserReader r, Object outer, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
160
161      if (eType == null)
162         eType = object();
163      var swap = (ObjectSwap<T,Object>)eType.getSwap(this);
164      var builder = (BuilderSwap<T,Object>)eType.getBuilderSwap(this);
165      ClassMeta<?> sType = null;
166      if (nn(builder))
167         sType = builder.getBuilderClassMeta(this);
168      else if (nn(swap))
169         sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
170      else
171         sType = eType;
172
173      if (sType.isOptional())
174         return (T)opt(parseAnything(eType.getElementType(), r, outer, pMeta));
175
176      setCurrentClass(sType);
177      var wrapperAttr = getJsonClassMeta(sType).getWrapperAttr();
178
179      Object o = null;
180
181      skipCommentsAndSpace(r);
182      if (nn(wrapperAttr))
183         skipWrapperAttrStart(r, wrapperAttr);
184      int c = r.peek();
185      if (c == -1) {
186         onEmptyInput();
187      } else if ((c == ',' || c == '}' || c == ']')) {
188         onMissingValue();
189      } else if (c == 'n') {
190         parseKeyword("null", r);
191      } else if (sType.isObject()) {
192         if (c == '{') {
193            var m2 = newGenericMap();
194            parseIntoMap2(r, m2, string(), object(), pMeta);
195            o = cast(m2, pMeta, eType);
196         } else if (c == '[') {
197            o = parseIntoCollection2(r, newGenericList(), object(), pMeta);
198         } else if (c == '\'' || c == '"') {
199            o = parseString(r);
200            if (sType.isChar())
201               o = parseCharacter(o);
202         } else if (c >= '0' && c <= '9' || c == '-' || c == '.') {
203            o = parseNumber(r, null);
204         } else if (c == 't') {
205            parseKeyword("true", r);
206            o = Boolean.TRUE;
207         } else {
208            parseKeyword("false", r);
209            o = Boolean.FALSE;
210         }
211      } else if (nn(builder)) {
212         var m = toBeanMap(builder.create(this, eType));
213         o = builder.build(this, parseIntoBeanMap2(r, m).getBean(), eType);
214      } else if (sType.canCreateNewBean(outer)) {
215         var m = newBeanMap(outer, sType.inner());
216         o = parseIntoBeanMap2(r, m).getBean();
217      } else if (sType.isMap()) {
218         Map m = (sType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map)sType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap(sType));
219         o = parseIntoMap2(r, m, sType.getKeyType(), sType.getValueType(), pMeta);
220      } else if (sType.isCollection()) {
221         if (c == '{') {
222            var m = newGenericMap();
223            parseIntoMap2(r, m, string(), object(), pMeta);
224            o = cast(m, pMeta, eType);
225         } else {
226            Collection l = (sType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Collection)sType.newInstance() : newGenericList());
227            o = parseIntoCollection2(r, l, sType, pMeta);
228         }
229      } else if (sType.isArray() || sType.isArgs()) {
230         if (c == '{') {
231            var m = newGenericMap();
232            parseIntoMap2(r, m, string(), object(), pMeta);
233            o = cast(m, pMeta, eType);
234         } else {
235            var l = (ArrayList)parseIntoCollection2(r, list(), sType, pMeta);
236            o = toArray(sType, l);
237         }
238      } else if (sType.isCharSequence()) {
239         o = parseString(r);
240      } else if (sType.isChar()) {
241         o = parseCharacter(parseString(r));
242      } else if (sType.isNumber()) {
243         o = parseNumber(r, (Class<? extends Number>)sType.inner());
244      } else if (sType.isBoolean()) {
245         o = parseBoolean(r);
246      } else if (sType.isDate()) {
247         o = parseDate(parseString(r), sType);
248      } else if (sType.isCalendar()) {
249         o = parseCalendar(parseString(r), sType);
250      } else if (sType.isTemporal()) {
251         o = parseTemporal(parseString(r), sType);
252      } else if (sType.isDuration()) {
253         o = parseDuration(parseString(r));
254      } else if (sType.isPeriod()) {
255         o = parsePeriod(parseString(r));
256      } else if (sType.canCreateNewInstanceFromString(outer) && (c == '\'' || c == '"')) {
257         o = sType.newInstanceFromString(outer, parseString(r));
258      } else if (c == '{') {
259         Map m = newGenericMap();
260         parseIntoMap2(r, m, sType.getKeyType(), sType.getValueType(), pMeta);
261         if (m.containsKey(getBeanTypePropertyName(eType)))
262            o = cast((MarshalledMap)m, pMeta, eType);
263         else if (nn(sType.getProxyInvocationHandler()))
264            o = newBeanMap(outer, sType.inner()).load(m).getBean();
265         else
266            throw new ParseException(this, "Class ''{0}'' could not be instantiated.  Reason: ''{1}''", cn(sType), sType.getNotABeanReason());
267      } else if (sType.canCreateNewInstanceFromString(outer) && canCoerceNonStringToString()) {
268         o = sType.newInstanceFromString(outer, parseString(r));
269      } else {
270         throw new ParseException(this, "Unrecognized syntax for class type ''{0}'', starting character ''{1}''", sType, (char)c);
271      }
272
273      if (nn(wrapperAttr))
274         skipWrapperAttrEnd(r);
275
276      if (nn(swap) && nn(o))
277         o = unswap(swap, o, eType);
278
279      if (nn(outer))
280         setParent(eType, o, outer);
281
282      return (T)o;
283   }
284
285   private Boolean parseBoolean(ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
286      int c = r.peek();
287      if (c == '\'' || c == '"')
288         return bool(parseString(r));
289      if (c == 't') {
290         parseKeyword("true", r);
291         return Boolean.TRUE;
292      } else if (c == 'f') {
293         parseKeyword("false", r);
294         return Boolean.FALSE;
295      } else {
296         throw new ParseException(this, "Unrecognized syntax.  Expected boolean value, actual=''{0}''", r.read(100));
297      }
298   }
299
300   /*
301    * Parse a JSON attribute from the character array at the specified position, then
302    * set the position marker to the last character in the field name.
303    */
304   protected String parseFieldName(ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
305      int c = r.peek();
306      if (c == '\'' || c == '"')
307         return parseString(r);
308      if (c == 'n') {
309         parseKeyword("null", r);
310         return null;
311      }
312      throw new ParseException(this, "Unquoted attribute detected.");
313   }
314
315   @SuppressWarnings({
316      "java:S1168",    // Compiler-satisfying return: all paths return m or throw. S1168 flags null returns; here null is unreachable.
317      "java:S2583",    // State variables persist across loop iterations
318      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parser state machine
319   })
320   private <T> BeanMap<T> parseIntoBeanMap2(ParserReader r, BeanMap<T> m) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
321
322      // S1: Looking for outer {
323      // S2: Looking for attrName start.
324      // S3: Found attrName end, looking for :.
325      // S4: Found :, looking for valStart: { [ " ' LITERAL.
326      // S5: Looking for , or }
327
328      var state = S1;
329      var currAttr = "";
330      int c = 0;
331      mark();
332      try {
333         while (c != -1) {
334            c = r.read();
335            if (state == S1) {
336               if (c == '{') {
337                  state = S2;
338               } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
339                  skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
340               } else {
341                  break;
342               }
343            } else if (state == S2) {
344               if (c == '}') {
345                  return m;
346               } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
347                  skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
348               } else {
349                  r.unread();
350                  mark();
351                  currAttr = parseFieldName(r);
352                  state = S3;
353               }
354            } else if (state == S3) {  // NOSONAR - State check necessary for state machine
355               if (c == ':')
356                  state = S4;
357            } else if (state == S4) {
358               if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
359                  skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
360               } else {
361               if (! currAttr.equals(getBeanTypePropertyName((ClassMeta<?>) m.getBeanInfo()))) {
362                  var pMeta = m.getPropertyMeta(currAttr);
363                  setCurrentProperty(pMeta);
364                  if (pMeta == null) {
365                     onUnknownProperty(currAttr, m, parseAnything(object(), r.unread(), m.getBean(false), null));
366                     unmark();
367                  } else {
368                     unmark();
369                     var cm = (ClassMeta<?>) pMeta.getBeanInfo();
370                     Object value = parseAnything(cm, r.unread(), m.getBean(false), pMeta);
371                     setName(cm, value, currAttr);
372                        try {
373                           pMeta.set(m, currAttr, value);
374                        } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
375                           onBeanSetterException(pMeta, e);
376                           throw e;
377                        }
378                     }
379                     setCurrentProperty(null);
380                  }
381                  state = S5;
382               }
383            } else if (state == S5) {
384               if (c == ',')
385                  state = S2;
386               else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c))
387                  skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
388               else if (c == '}') {
389                  return m;
390               }
391            }
392         }
393         if (state == S1)
394            throw new ParseException(this, "Expected '{' at beginning of JSON object.");
395         else if (state == S2)
396            throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find attribute name on JSON object.");
397         else if (state == S3)
398            throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find ':' following attribute name on JSON object.");
399         else if (state == S4)
400            throw new ParseException(this, "Expected one of the following characters: {,[,',\",LITERAL.");
401         else if (state == S5)
402            throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find '}' marking end of JSON object.");
403      } finally {
404         unmark();
405      }
406
407      return null; // Unreachable.
408   }
409
410   @SuppressWarnings({
411      "java:S1168",    // Compiler-satisfying return: all paths return l or throw. S1168 flags null returns; here null is unreachable.
412      "java:S135",     // Multiple break statements necessary for state machine error machine
413      "java:S2583",    // State variables persist across loop iterations
414      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parser state machine
415   })
416   private <E> Collection<E> parseIntoCollection2(ParserReader r, Collection<E> l, ClassMeta<?> type, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
417
418      // S1: Looking for outermost [
419      // S2: Looking for starting [ or { or " or ' or LITERAL or ]
420      // S3: Looking for , or ]
421      // S4: Looking for starting [ or { or " or ' or LITERAL
422
423      int argIndex = 0;
424
425      var state = S1;
426      int c = 0;
427      while (c != -1) {
428         c = r.read();
429         if (state == S1) {
430            if (c == '[')
431               state = S2;
432            else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c))
433               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
434            else
435               break;  // Invalid character found.
436         } else if (state == S2) {
437            if (c == ']') {
438               return l;
439            } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
440               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
441            } else if (c != -1) {
442               l.add((E)parseAnything(type.isArgs() ? type.getArg(argIndex++) : type.getElementType(), r.unread(), l, pMeta));
443               state = S3;
444            }
445         } else if (state == S3) {  // NOSONAR - State check necessary for state machine
446            if (c == ',') {
447               state = S4;
448            } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
449               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
450            } else if (c == ']') {
451               return l;
452            } else {
453               break;  // Invalid character found.
454            }
455         } else if (state == S4) {
456            if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
457               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
458            } else if (c == ']') {
459               break;
460            } else if (c != -1) {
461               l.add((E)parseAnything(type.isArgs() ? type.getArg(argIndex++) : type.getElementType(), r.unread(), l, pMeta));
462               state = S3;
463            }
464         }
465      }
466      if (state == S1)
467         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected '[' at beginning of JSON array.");
468      else if (state == S2)
469         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected one of the following characters: {,[,',\",LITERAL.");
470      else if (state == S3)
471         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected ',' or ']'.");
472      else if (state == S4)
473         throw new ParseException(this, "Unexpected trailing comma in array.");
474
475      return null;  // Unreachable.
476   }
477
478   @SuppressWarnings({
479      "java:S1168",    // Compiler-satisfying return: all paths return m or throw. S1168 flags null returns; here null is unreachable.
480      "java:S135",     // Multiple break statements necessary for state machine error handling
481      "java:S2583",    // State variables persist across loop iterations
482      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parser state machine
483   })
484   private <K,V> Map<K,V> parseIntoMap2(ParserReader r, Map<K,V> m, ClassMeta<K> keyType, ClassMeta<V> valueType, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
485
486      if (keyType == null)
487         keyType = (ClassMeta<K>)string();
488
489      // S1: Looking for outer {
490      // S2: Looking for attrName start.
491      // S3: Found attrName end, looking for :.
492      // S4: Found :, looking for valStart: { [ " ' LITERAL.
493      // S5: Looking for , or }
494      // S6: Found , looking for attr start.
495
496      skipCommentsAndSpace(r);
497      var state = S1;
498      String currAttr = null;
499      int c = 0;
500      while (c != -1) {
501         c = r.read();
502         if (state == S1) {
503            if (c == '{')
504               state = S2;
505            else
506               break;
507         } else if (state == S2) {
508            if (c == '}') {
509               return m;
510            } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
511               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
512            } else {
513               currAttr = parseFieldName(r.unread());
514               state = S3;
515            }
516         } else if (state == S3) {  // NOSONAR - State check necessary for state machine
517            if (c == ':')
518               state = S4;
519         } else if (state == S4) {
520            if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
521               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
522            } else {
523               K key = convertAttrToType(m, currAttr, keyType);
524               V value = parseAnything(valueType, r.unread(), m, pMeta);
525               setName(valueType, value, key);
526               m.put(key, value);
527               state = S5;
528            }
529         } else if (state == S5) {
530            if (c == ',') {
531               state = S6;
532            } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
533               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
534            } else if (c == '}') {
535               return m;
536            } else {
537               break;
538            }
539         } else if (state == S6) {
540            if (c == '}') {
541               break;
542            } else if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
543               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
544            } else {
545               currAttr = parseFieldName(r.unread());
546               state = S3;
547            }
548         }
549      }
550      if (state == S1)
551         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected '{' at beginning of JSON object.");
552      else if (state == S2)
553         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find attribute name on JSON object.");
554      else if (state == S3)
555         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find ':' following attribute name on JSON object.");
556      else if (state == S4)
557         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected one of the following characters: {,[,',\",LITERAL.");
558      else if (state == S5)
559         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find '}' marking end of JSON object.");
560      else if (state == S6)
561         throw new ParseException(this, "Unexpected '}' found in JSON object.");
562
563      return null; // Unreachable.
564   }
565
566   /*
567    * Looks for the keywords true, false, or null.
568    * Throws an exception if any of these keywords are not found at the specified position.
569    */
570   protected void parseKeyword(String keyword, ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
571      try {
572         String s = r.read(keyword.length());
573         if (s.equals(keyword))
574            return;
575         throw new ParseException(this, "Unrecognized syntax.  Expected=''{0}'', Actual=''{1}''", keyword, s);
576      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IndexOutOfBoundsException e) {
577         throw new ParseException(this, "Unrecognized syntax.  Expected=''{0}'', found end-of-file.", keyword);
578      }
579   }
580
581   protected Number parseNumber(ParserReader r, Class<? extends Number> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
582      int c = r.peek();
583      if (c == '\'' || c == '"')
584         return parseNumber(r, parseString(r), type);
585      return parseNumber(r, r.parseNumberString(), type);
586   }
587
588   @SuppressWarnings({
589      "unused",    // r accepted for API consistency; subclasses may use it for position tracking
590      "java:S1172", // Same as above
591      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for number parsing logic
592   })
593   protected Number parseNumber(ParserReader r, String s, Class<? extends Number> type) throws ParseException {
594
595      if (s.isEmpty())
596         throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid JSON number: ''{0}''", s);
597
598      var isNegative = false;
599      var c = s.charAt(0);
600      if (c == '-') {
601         isNegative = true;
602         c = (s.length() == 1 ? 'x' : s.charAt(1));
603      }
604
605      if (c == '.')
606         throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid JSON number: ''{0}''", s);
607
608      if (c == '0' && s.length() > (isNegative ? 2 : 1)) {
609         var c2 = s.charAt((isNegative ? 2 : 1));
610         if (c2 != '.' && c2 != 'e' && c2 != 'E')
611            throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid JSON number: ''{0}''", s);
612      }
613
614      var i = s.indexOf('.');
615      if (i != -1 && (s.length() == (i + 1) || ! decChars.contains(s.charAt(i + 1))))
616         throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid JSON number: ''{0}''", s);
617
618      return StringUtils.parseNumber(s, type);
619   }
620
621   /*
622    * Starting from the specified position in the character array, returns the
623    * position of the character " or '.
624    * If the string consists of a concatenation of strings (e.g. 'AAA' + "BBB"), this method
625    * will automatically concatenate the strings and return the result.
626    */
627   @SuppressWarnings({
628      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for parser state machine
629      "java:S135" // Multiple break statements necessary for state machine error handling
630   })
631   protected String parseString(ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
632      r.mark();
633      int qc = r.read();
634      if (qc != '"') {
635         var msg = (qc == '\'' ? "Invalid quote character \"{0}\" being used." : "Did not find quote character marking beginning of string.  Character=\"{0}\"");
636         throw new ParseException(this, msg, (char)qc);
637      }
638      String s = null;
639      boolean isInEscape = false;
640      int c = 0;
641      while (c != -1) {
642         c = r.read();
643         if (c <= 0x1F)
644            throw new ParseException(this, "Unescaped control character encountered: ''0x{0}''", String.format("%04X", c));
645         if (isInEscape) {
646            // @formatter:off
647            switch (c) {
648               case 'n': r.replace('\n'); break;
649               case 'r': r.replace('\r'); break;
650               case 't': r.replace('\t'); break;
651               case 'f': r.replace('\f'); break;
652               case 'b': r.replace('\b'); break;
653               case '\\': r.replace('\\'); break;
654               case '/': r.replace('/'); break;
655               case '\'': r.replace('\''); break;
656               case '"': r.replace('"'); break;
657               case 'u': {
658                  String n = r.read(4);
659                  try {
660                     r.replace(Integer.parseInt(n, 16), 6);
661                  } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
662                     throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid Unicode escape sequence in string.");
663                  }
664                  break;
665               }
666               default:
667                  throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid escape sequence in string.");
668            }
669            // @formatter:on
670            isInEscape = false;
671         } else {
672            if (c == '\\') {
673               isInEscape = true;
674               r.delete();
675            } else if (c == qc) {
676               s = r.getMarked(1, -1);
677               break;
678            }
679         }
680      }
681      if (s == null)
682         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find expected end character ''{0}''.", (char)qc);
683
684      skipCommentsAndSpace(r);
685      if (r.peek() == '+')
686         throw new ParseException(this, "String concatenation detected.");
687      return trim(s);
688   }
689
690   /*
691    * Doesn't actually parse anything, but when positioned at the beginning of comment,
692    * it will move the pointer to the last character in the comment.
693    */
694   @SuppressWarnings({
695      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for comment parsing logic
696   })
697   protected void skipComments(ParserReader r) throws ParseException, IOException {
698      int c = r.read();
699      if (c == '*') {
700         while (c != -1)
701            if ((c = r.read()) == '*' && (c = r.read()) == '/')
702               return;
703      } else if (c == '/') {
704         while (c != -1) {
705            c = r.read();
706            if (c == -1 || c == '\n')
707               return;
708         }
709      }
710      throw new ParseException(this, "Open ended comment.");
711   }
712
713   protected void skipCommentsAndSpace(ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
714      int c = 0;
715      while ((c = r.read()) != -1) {
716         if (! isWhitespace(c)) {
717            if (c == '/')
718               throw new ParseException(this, "Javascript comment detected.");
719            r.unread();
720            return;
721         }
722      }
723   }
724
725   /*
726    * Doesn't actually parse anything, but moves the position beyond the construct "}" when
727    * the @Json(wrapperAttr) annotation is used on a class.
728    */
729   private void skipWrapperAttrEnd(ParserReader r) throws ParseException, IOException {
730      int c = 0;
731      while ((c = r.read()) != -1) {
732         if (! isWhitespace(c)) {
733            if (c == '/') {
734               throw new ParseException(this, "Javascript comment detected.");
735            } else if (c == '}') {
736               return;
737            } else {
738               throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find '}' at the end of JSON wrapper object.");
739            }
740         }
741      }
742   }
743
744   /*
745    * Doesn't actually parse anything, but moves the position beyond the construct "{wrapperAttr:" when
746    * the @Json(wrapperAttr) annotation is used on a class.
747    */
748   @SuppressWarnings({
749      "java:S2583",    // State variables persist across loop iterations
750      "java:S3776"     // Cognitive complexity acceptable for wrapper attribute parsing
751   })
752   private void skipWrapperAttrStart(ParserReader r, String wrapperAttr) throws IOException, ParseException {
753
754      // S1: Looking for outer '{'
755      // S2: Looking for attrName start.
756      // S3: Found attrName end, looking for :.
757      // S4: Found :, looking for valStart: { [ " ' LITERAL.
758
759      var state = S1;
760      String currAttr = null;
761      int c = 0;
762      while (c != -1) {
763         c = r.read();
764         if (state == S1) {
765            if (c == '{')
766               state = S2;
767         } else if (state == S2) {
768            if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
769               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
770            } else {
771               currAttr = parseFieldName(r.unread());
772               if (! currAttr.equals(wrapperAttr))
773                  throw new ParseException(this, "Expected to find wrapper attribute ''{0}'' but found attribute ''{1}''", wrapperAttr, currAttr);
774               state = S3;
775            }
776         } else if (state == S3) {  // NOSONAR - State check necessary for state machine
777            if (c == ':')
778               state = S4;
779         } else if (state == S4) {
780            if (isCommentOrWhitespace(c)) {
781               skipCommentsAndSpace(r.unread());
782            } else {
783               r.unread();
784               return;
785            }
786         }
787      }
788      if (state == S1)
789         throw new ParseException(this, "Expected '{' at beginning of JSON object.");
790      else if (state == S2)
791         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find attribute name on JSON object.");
792      else
793         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not find ':' following attribute name on JSON object.");
794   }
795
796   /*
797    * Call this method after you've finished a parsing a string to make sure that if there's any
798    * remainder in the input, that it consists only of whitespace and comments.
799    */
800   private void validateEnd(ParserReader r) throws IOException, ParseException {
801      if (! isValidateEnd())
802         return;
803      skipCommentsAndSpace(r);
804      int c = r.read();
805      if (c != -1 && c != ';')  // var x = {...}; expressions can end with a semicolon.
806         throw new ParseException(this, "Remainder after parse: ''{0}''.", (char)c);
807   }
808
809   @Override /* Overridden from ParserSession */
810   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
811      try (var r = pipe.getParserReader()) {
812         if (r == null)
813            return null;
814         T o = parseAnything(type, r, getOuter(), null);
815         validateEnd(r);
816         return o;
817      }
818   }
819
820   /**
821    * Opens a low-level pull-parser cursor over a JSON document, bound to this session's live
822    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanSession}/{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext}.
823    *
824    * <p>
825    * The cursor is purely structural &mdash; object swaps and {@code @Schema} annotations are not
826    * applied at this layer.  See {@link TokenReader} for the contract.
827    *
828    * <h5 class='section'>Builder properties honored:</h5>
829    * The structurally-applicable subset flows through to the cursor: <c>autoCloseStreams</c>,
830    * <c>unbuffered</c>, <c>trimStrings</c> (applied to {@link TokenType#VALUE_STRING} and
831    * {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME} values), and the {@code ReaderParser} stream/file charsets.
832    *
833    * <h5 class='section'>Builder properties NOT honored:</h5>
834    * <c>validateEnd()</c> (the cursor leaves end-of-input checking to the caller),
835    * <c>listener(...)</c>/<c>debugOutputLines(...)</c>, <c>consumes(...)</c>, object swaps, and
836    * {@code @Schema} annotations (only applied on the POJO databind path).
837    *
838    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence}, {@link InputStream},
839    *    <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
840    * @return A new {@link JsonTokenReader}.
841    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
842    */
843   @SuppressWarnings({
844      "java:S2095" // ParserPipe lifecycle is transferred to the returned JsonTokenReader, which closes it via its own close(); the caller owns the cursor via try-with-resources.
845   })
846   @Override /* TokenReadable */
847   public TokenReader parseTokens(Object input) throws IOException {
848      var pipe = new ParserPipe(
849         input,
850         isDebug(),
851         false /* strict */,
852         isAutoCloseStreams(),
853         isUnbuffered(),
854         getStreamCharset(),
855         getFileCharset());
856      return new JsonTokenReader(pipe, new JsonTokenReader.Settings(isTrimStrings()), this);
857   }
858
859   /**
860    * Streaming array-element {@link RecordReader} backed by {@link JsonTokenReader}.
861    * Memory is O(1) in the array length &mdash; elements are bound on demand using this live session.
862    *
863    * @param input The input.
864    * @return A new element-streamed {@link RecordReader}.
865    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
866    */
867   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
868   public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
869      try {
870         return StreamingArrayRecord.reader(parseTokens(input));
871      } catch (ParseException e) {
872         throw new IOException(e);
873      }
874   }
875
876   @Override /* Overridden from ReaderParserSession */
877   protected <E> Collection<E> doParseIntoCollection(ParserPipe pipe, Collection<E> c, Type elementType) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
878      try (var r = pipe.getParserReader()) {
879         c = parseIntoCollection2(r, c, getClassMeta(elementType == null ? Object.class : elementType), null);
880         validateEnd(r);
881         return c;
882      }
883   }
884
885   @Override /* Overridden from ReaderParserSession */
886   protected <K,V> Map<K,V> doParseIntoMap(ParserPipe pipe, Map<K,V> m, Type keyType, Type valueType) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
887      try (var r = pipe.getParserReader()) {
888         m = parseIntoMap2(r, m, (ClassMeta<K>)getClassMeta(keyType == null ? Object.class : keyType), (ClassMeta<V>)getClassMeta(valueType == null ? Object.class : valueType), null);
889         validateEnd(r);
890         return m;
891      }
892   }
893
894   /**
895    * Returns the language-specific metadata on the specified class.
896    *
897    * @param cm The class to return the metadata on.
898    * @return The metadata.
899    */
900   protected JsonClassMeta getJsonClassMeta(ClassMeta<?> cm) {
901      return ctx.getJsonClassMeta(cm);
902   }
903
904   /**
905    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified character is whitespace or '/'.
906    *
907    * @param cp The codepoint.
908    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified character is whitespace or '/'.
909    */
910   protected boolean isCommentOrWhitespace(int cp) {
911      if (cp == '/')
912         return true;
913      return cp <= 0x20 && (cp == 0x09 || cp == 0x0A || cp == 0x0D || cp == 0x20);
914   }
915
916   /**
917    * Validate end.
918    *
919    * @see JsonParser.Builder#validateEnd()
920    * @return
921    *    <jk>true</jk> if after parsing a POJO from the input, verifies that the remaining input in
922    *    the stream consists of only comments or whitespace.
923    */
924   protected boolean isValidateEnd() { return validateEnd; }
925
926   /**
927    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified character is whitespace.
928    *
929    * <p>
930    * Only 0x20 (space), 0x09 (tab), 0x0A (line feed) and 0x0D (carriage return) are considered whitespace.
931    *
932    * @param cp The codepoint.
933    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified character is whitespace.
934    */
935   protected boolean isWhitespace(int cp) {
936      return cp <= 0x20 && (cp == 0x09 || cp == 0x0A || cp == 0x0D || cp == 0x20);
937   }
938
939   /**
940    * Hook called when empty input is detected in {@code parseAnything()}.
941    *
942    * @throws ParseException Always in strict mode.
943    */
944   protected void onEmptyInput() throws ParseException {
945      throw new ParseException(this, "Empty input.");
946   }
947
948   /**
949    * Hook called when a missing value is detected in {@code parseAnything()}.
950    *
951    * @throws ParseException Always in strict mode.
952    */
953   protected void onMissingValue() throws ParseException {
954      throw new ParseException(this, "Missing value detected.");
955   }
956
957   /**
958    * Returns whether non-string types can be coerced to strings using {@code newInstanceFromString()}.
959    *
960    * @return Always {@code false} for standard JSON.
961    */
962   protected boolean canCoerceNonStringToString() {
963      return false;
964   }
965
966   @SuppressWarnings({
967      "java:S6466" // False positive: new JsonMap(this) resolves to JsonMap(MarshallingSession), not JsonMap(Object...), since JsonParserSession IS-A MarshallingSession.
968   })
969   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingSession */
970   protected MarshalledMap newGenericMap() {
971      return new JsonMap(this);
972   }
973
974   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingSession */
975   protected MarshalledList newGenericList() {
976      return new JsonList(this);
977   }
978}
